title: Hedeoma bella
intro: Hedeoma bella, also known as Wood's rose or beauty mountain mint, is a flowering plant in the mint family (Lamiaceae) native to the southwestern United States. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which may help alleviate symptoms of colds, sore throats, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Hedeoma bella include essential oils, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, such as rosmarinic acid and ursolic acid, which contribute to its therapeutic benefits. In herbal medicine, Hedeoma bella is often prepared as a tea or tincture, as well as being used in topical applications for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
taxonomy: Hedeoma bella is classified within the family Lamiaceae, commonly known as the mint family. It belongs to the genus Hedeoma, which consists of annual or perennial herbs native to North America. The genus Hedeoma is further divided into several species, with Hedeoma bella being one of them. This classification places Hedeoma bella within the clade of flowering plants and the larger clade of angiosperms. As a member of the Lamiaceae family, Hedeoma bella shares characteristics with other mint family plants.
